questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: To provide entertainment videos for Notion users
options.explanation: The article does not mention entertainment; it focuses on learning.

options.text: To turn curiosity into capability by teaching Notion and offering badges and certifications
options.explanation: The headline explicitly says “Turn curiosity into capability — learn Notion, build smarter workflows, and validate your skills with badges & certifications.”

options.text: To sell third‑party templates in the Notion Marketplace
options.explanation: Selling templates is mentioned as a lesson topic, not the overall purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To provide one‑on‑one consulting services for enterprises
options.explanation: Consulting services are not discussed in the article.

questions.question: Which of the following items can you earn by completing a Notion Academy learning path?

options.explanation: The admin certification is a separate program, not automatically granted by a learning path.
The article states you can “earn badges with Notion” by completing paths.
No monetary rewards are mentioned.
Only digital badges and certifications are referenced.

questions.question: Which lesson is labeled as Intermediate and focuses on building an intake system using forms, charts, and automations?

options.explanation: Custom Agents is a Beginner‑level lesson about automation, not an intake system.

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: The description matches the Intermediate lesson that teaches an intake system.
That lesson is Beginner‑level and focuses on teamwork, not intake systems.
Workspace design is an Advanced lesson about design principles.

questions.question: How many individual lessons are included in the Essentials Path?

options.explanation: The Essentials Path is described as “3 Lessons.”
Four lessons belong to the Workflows Path, not the Essentials Path.
Five lessons are part of the Advanced Path.
The article does not list a two‑lesson path.

questions.question: Which learning path explicitly mentions the use of AI to help create connected systems?

options.explanation: The Essentials Path focuses on basic skills and does not reference AI.
The description says “Create connected systems that unite teams and projects, with the help of AI.”
The Advanced Path emphasizes automation and architecture but does not specifically call out AI.
Custom Agents is a single lesson, not a path, and it focuses on automation rather than AI‑assisted system creation.

questions.question: If you want to learn best practices for designing a Notion workspace, which lesson should you select and what is its difficulty level?

options.explanation: The lesson is listed as Advanced, not Beginner.
The article tags “Workspace design” as an Advanced lesson.
Custom Agents deals with automation, not workspace design.
While foundational, this lesson covers navigation and block creation, not workspace design best practices.

questions.question: Which lesson has the shortest listed duration, and what is its primary focus?

options.text: "Workspace design" – 10 minutes – best practices for designing your workspace
options.explanation: The article lists “Workspace design” as a 10‑minute Advanced lesson about workspace design.
The duration is longer (30 min) and the focus is automation, not the shortest.
While short, it is longer than the 10‑minute lesson.

options.text: "Building basics" – 20 minutes – navigating and creating blocks
options.explanation: This lesson runs 20 min, which is not the shortest.

questions.question: Which certification can you obtain regardless of the Notion plan your organization uses?

options.explanation: The article states the admin certification is available “for any plan type at any company.”
A Power User badge is mentioned, but it is not described as a certification available to all plan types.
No such certification is referenced in the article.
The article does not discuss a developer certification.
